optionList.content: Bowel movement with soft, formed stool
Bowel movement with soft, formed stool

studentQuestionArray.content: The nurse has administered a dose of docusate. The nurse evaluates that the medication has been effective if the client experiences which finding?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationale:
Docusate is a stool softener that relieves constipation because it promotes absorption of water into the stool, producing a softer consistency of stool. The other options are not effects of this medication.

studentQuestionArray.content: A client taking an oral laxative wants to obtain a rapid effect from the medication. How would the nurse instruct the client to take the medication?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationale:
Most rapid results from an oral laxative occur when it is taken on an empty stomach. If taken at bedtime, the client will have a bowel movement in the morning. It will not have a rapid effect if taken with a meal.

optionList.content: I will take it at bedtime.
I will take it with a large meal.
I will take it on an empty stomach.
I will drink two glasses of milk when I take it.
I will take it at bedtime.
I will take it with a large meal.
I will take it on an empty stomach.
I will drink two glasses of milk when I take it.

studentQuestionArray.content: A client with constipation has been directed to take oral bisacodyl. The nurse instructs the client on how to receive the most rapid effect from the medication. The client demonstrates understanding of use of this medication by stating that which method will produce the most rapid effect?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationale:
The most rapid results from bisacodyl occur when it is taken on an empty stomach. If it is taken at bedtime, the client will have a bowel movement in the morning. It will not have a rapid effect if taken with a large meal or with two glasses of milk.

studentQuestionArray.content: A client has begun taking a stimulant laxative. In monitoring the client for medication side and adverse effects, the nurse is likely to note which finding?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationale:
A stimulant laxative causes nausea and abdominal cramps as the most frequent side effects. The incorrect options represent health problems that are not caused by this medication.

optionList.content: A total of two glasses of liquid
A total of two glasses of liquid

studentQuestionArray.content: A client has been told by a primary health care provider to take psyllium daily. The nurse would tell the client to take the medication with which item?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationale:
Psyllium is a bulk-forming laxative that needs to be taken with a full glass of water or juice, followed by another glass of liquid. This will help prevent impaction of the medication in the stomach or small intestine. The other options are incorrect.

studentQuestionArray.content: The nurse is preparing to administer prescribed medications to a client with hepatic encephalopathy. The nurse anticipates that the primary health care provider's prescriptions will include which medication?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationale:
Lactulose is a hyperosmotic laxative agent that has the adjunct benefit of lowering serum ammonia levels. This occurs because the medication lowers bowel pH and aids in the conversion of ammonia in the gut to the ammonium ion, which is poorly absorbed. Bisacodyl is a stimulant laxative. Magnesium hydroxide is a saline laxative. Psyllium hydrophilic mucilloid is a bulk laxative.

optionList.content: The client has peptic ulcer disease.
The client is experiencing a case of influenza.
This is a common side effect of this medication.
The client may have a partial bowel obstruction.
The client has peptic ulcer disease.
The client is experiencing a case of influenza.
This is a common side effect of this medication.
The client may have a partial bowel obstruction.

studentQuestionArray.content: A client who is taking a stimulant laxative develops abdominal cramps. The nurse interprets that this clinical manifestation most likely indicates the presence of which problem?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationale:
Stimulant laxatives commonly cause abdominal cramps as a side effect. The health problems noted in the other options are not determined based on a single symptom.

optionList.content: I will administer the mineral oil before each meal.
I will administer the mineral oil followed by a glass of warm water.
I will mix the mineral oil with a chilled drink before administration.
I will mix the mineral oil with 8 ounces of warm juice before administration.
I will administer the mineral oil before each meal.
I will administer the mineral oil followed by a glass of warm water.
I will mix the mineral oil with a chilled drink before administration.
I will mix the mineral oil with 8 ounces of warm juice before administration.

studentQuestionArray.content: Mineral oil has been prescribed for constipation in a pediatric client, and the nurse teaches about administration of the mineral oil. Which statement by the parent indicates that teaching was effective?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationale:
Mineral oil is best tolerated when it is given chilled or mixed with cold drinks. Mixing the oil with chocolate milk, blending it with ice cubes and fruit juice, or chilling it helps to disguise the taste. Administering mineral oil before meals would affect appetite.

studentQuestionArray.content: A client is taking docusate sodium. Which finding by the nurse indicates that treatment has been effective?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationale:

Docusate sodium is a stool softener that promotes absorption of water into the stool, producing a softer consistency of stool. The intended effect is relief or prevention of constipation. The medication does not decrease the amount of fat in the stools, stop gastrointestinal bleeding, or relieve abdominal pain.

studentQuestionArray.content: The adult client with hepatic encephalopathy has a serum ammonia level of 200 mcg/dL (120 mcmol/L) and receives treatment with lactulose. The nurse determines that the client had the best and most realistic response if the serum ammonia level changed to which value after medication administration?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationale:
The normal serum ammonia level is 10 to 80 mcg/dL (6 to 47 mcmol/L). In the client with hepatic encephalopathy, the ammonia level is not likely to drop below normal, nor is it likely to drop into the low-normal range. A level of 90 mcg/dL (54 mcmol/L) is slightly above normal and represents the most realistic response of the medication. The nurse would also monitor the client for signs and symptoms that indicate improvement in the condition.

studentQuestionArray.content: A client with portosystemic encephalopathy is receiving oral lactulose daily. The nurse would check which item to determine that this medication has been effective?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationale:
Lactulose is a hyperosmotic laxative and ammonia detoxicant. It is used to prevent or treat portosystemic encephalopathy, including hepatic precoma and coma. It also is used to treat constipation. The medication retains ammonia in the colon (decreases the blood ammonia concentration), producing an osmotic effect. It promotes increased peristalsis and bowel evacuation, expelling ammonia from the colon. This medication has no effect on lung sounds, blood pressure, or serum potassium level.

optionList.content: With a full glass of water
After it is chilled in the refrigerator
With a full glass of water
After it is chilled in the refrigerator

studentQuestionArray.content: A client has a prescription for magnesium citrate to prevent constipation after undergoing barium studies of the gastrointestinal tract. How would the nurse administer the magnesium citrate?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationale:
Magnesium citrate is available as an oral solution and would be served chilled to make it more palatable. The other options are incorrect.

optionList.content: It lines the wall of the bowel.
It adds fiber and bulk to the stool.
It stimulates the vagus nerve to improve bowel tone.
It lines the wall of the bowel.
It adds fiber and bulk to the stool.
It stimulates the vagus nerve to improve bowel tone.

studentQuestionArray.content: The nurse is administering senna to an older client. What is the expected result of this medication?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationale:
Senna works by altering the transport of water and electrolytes in the large intestine, which causes accumulation of water in the mass of stool and increased peristalsis. The other options are incorrect actions for this medication.

studentQuestionArray.content: Lactulose is prescribed for a client with a diagnosis of hepatic encephalopathy. The nurse would determine that this medication is effective if serum diagnostics reveal which finding?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationale:
Lactulose is prescribed for the client with hepatic encephalopathy to reduce bacterial breakdown of protein in the bowel. The medication creates an acidic environment in the bowel and causes the ammonia to leave the bloodstream and enter the colon. Ammonia then becomes trapped in the bowel. Lactulose also has a laxative effect that allows for the elimination of the ammonia.

optionList.content: Mix the medication with a full glass of water or juice.
Decrease fluid intake following administration of the medication.
Decrease the amount of fiber in the diet when taking this medication.
Mix the medication with a full glass of water or juice.
Decrease fluid intake following administration of the medication.
Decrease the amount of fiber in the diet when taking this medication.

studentQuestionArray.content: A client who has had a myocardial infarction has a prescription to take a powdered form of psyllium after discharge. The nurse would plan to include which information when teaching the client about this medication?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationale:
Psyllium is a bulk-forming laxative that should be taken with a full glass of water or juice (not applesauce), followed by another glass of liquid. This will help prevent impaction of the medication in the stomach or small intestine. Fiber in the diet and fluid intake would not be decreased unless specifically prescribed by the primary health care provider.

studentQuestionArray.content: An older client takes a stimulant laxative for ongoing management of chronic constipation. Which findings would the nurse expect to note when reviewing the client's laboratory results?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationale:
Hypokalemia can result from long-term use of a stimulant laxative. The medication stimulates peristalsis and alters fluid and electrolyte transport, thus helping fluid to accumulate in the colon. The remaining options are not specifically associated with the use of this medication.

optionList.content: Sprinkle the powder on top of a hot beverage.
Mix the powder with warm applesauce.
Mix the powder with a full glass of water or juice followed by drinking an additional glass of liquid.
Sprinkle the powder on top of a hot beverage.
Mix the powder with warm applesauce.
Mix the powder with a full glass of water or juice followed by drinking an additional glass of liquid.

studentQuestionArray.content: A client with recurrent constipation has been prescribed psyllium, powdered form. Teaching provided by the nurse would include which instruction?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationale:
Psyllium is a bulk-forming laxative. The powder form would be mixed with 8 oz of water or juice, followed by drinking another 8 oz of liquid. This will help prevent impaction of the medication in the stomach or small intestine. It is also available in a wafer form. The other options are incorrect methods of administration.
